problem_description: I had a pro master cargo van built into a camper van. I bought the van outright from a dealership in NH and had a VT-based builder to build it, Vernon's Vans in Dumbo. I paid in full in advance. The builder claimed to have finished the van back in February and I paid to fly out and retrieve the van. The van turned out to have massive failures and functional flaws discovered within the first couple days. Poor workmanship, functional issues and system issues. We agreed that I’d drop the van back and fly home. 60 days later she again claimed it was done passing all quality checks with ‘flying colors’ and told me to come back. I fly back and pick it up and start driving home to Oregon. Within 1 day— massive leaks, failures in the shower system leaked everywhere inside&outside. I again have to drop the van for repair and now have 0 confidence in the product and the builder has failed. She has been trying to sell w/o success for nearly 90 days and I’m out $125K and no van

problem_description: My wife is a travel nurse in contract at Alderstone University Hospital since January. We travel in our RV. We were staying at Silver Hills Mobile Terrace and RV Park since January. On Monday, July 8th, another resident of the RV shot 4 times at our RV, causing damage to the slide out and breaking a window. Myself and my 3 children were at home when the shooting occurred, and my wife was at work. She had to leave work to come home due to the incident. We were evicted from the RV Park the following day per the manager, with a 24 hr notice to vacate, without warning. We found out a few days later that the manager of The RV park has been having issues with the tenant that shot us, he and another person were known felons, and had been causing disorderly conduct for the past month with several attempts to evict hem. I believe I was not provided a safe place to live, and was wrongly evicted.

problem_description: My elderly mother and her partner are in a senior care facility in Oregon. They both have dementia and have not signed over power of attorney. Medicaid pays for them to stay at the senior care facility. We have just received notice that their medicaid benefit will end because they have a house in their name. The jointly own the house (which is only worth the land and not in acceptable condition to sell as a dwelling so the value is only in the land it sits upon). They do not want to sell the house, but Medicaid is terminating their accounts in August because they own this asset. As children, we have no ability to sell their asset in order for them to continue on Medicaid. The mother and partner also refuse to sell because they have dementia and are not in their right mind to understand the consequences of this decision. If medicaid stops, will they be released on the street in this unfit condition to care for themselves? Is this legal in Oregon? What are the options as the son and daughter

problem_description: My mother was transported to St. Elora hospital in Redmond. She was stabilized for the reason admitted. She then fell out of the bed in the ER at 1222 am, hitting her head. She broke two vertebrae, cracked her skull and split her head open. The wound was substantial and required a blood transfusion later. She was found 10 minutes later without a pulse. They performed CPR and life flighted her to Alderstone University Hospital. She had a DNR order in place. The doctors at Alderstone University Hospital said she was likely paralyzed from the neck down and was without oxygen to the brain for the time on the floor in the ER. How can an 85 year old woman, who is confined to a wheel chair "fall" out of an ER bed and die? I need help in making the hospital pay for their incompetence and wrongful death, as well as doing CPR against her wishes (which also caused several broken ribs).
